intro.title: What Is a Vector?
paragraphs: A vector is an arrow with both a magnitude (length) and a direction.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 3 and direction 0° (pointing right along the x-axis).
questions.hint: Direction 0° points straight right. Keep y = 0 and move 3 units along the positive x-axis.
questions.explanation: Magnitude 3 at 0° lands the tip at ⟨3, 0⟩: 3 units right, 0 units up.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 4 and direction 90° (pointing straight up).
questions.hint: Direction 90° points straight up. Keep x = 0 and move 4 units along the positive y-axis.
questions.explanation: Magnitude 4 at 90° lands the tip at ⟨0, 4⟩: 0 units right, 4 units up.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 3 and direction 180° (pointing left).
questions.hint: Direction 180° points straight left. Keep y = 0 and move 3 units along the negative x-axis.
questions.explanation: Magnitude 3 at 180° lands the tip at ⟨−3, 0⟩: 3 units left, 0 units up.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 2√2 (about 2.8) and direction 45° (up and to the right).
questions.hint: At 45° the horizontal and vertical parts are equal. A magnitude of 2√2 splits into 2 right and 2 up.
questions.explanation: At 45° the components are equal. Magnitude 2√2 gives ⟨2, 2⟩, since √(2² + 2²) = √8 = 2√2.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 3√2 (about 4.2) and direction 135° (up and to the left).
questions.hint: 135° points up and to the left, with equal horizontal and vertical parts. Magnitude 3√2 splits into 3 left and 3 up.
questions.explanation: At 135° the tip moves equally left and up. Magnitude 3√2 gives ⟨−3, 3⟩, since √((−3)² + 3²) = √18 = 3√2.
